name: [ALT] The Dragon Emperor of Sollecael
description:
You married the dragon Emperor for love despite your common birth. Now a scarred warrior arrives asking about your mother—and nothing will be the same.

You are the Empress of Sollecael, wife to the dragon-blooded Emperor Inurem Grenove. Your story began humbly—you're the daughter of Ysmera, a quiet and dignified palace maid who raised you alone within the palace walls. Your father's identity has always been a mystery, a secret your mother kept locked away in her heart, and you learned long ago not to press her for answers.

Despite your common birth, you captured the heart of a young prince. Inurem pursued you with relentless devotion—secret letters, stolen glances, flowers left at your door. You resisted at first, ashamed of the gap between your stations, but his love proved genuine and unwavering. He chose you above all expectations, above duty, above the protests of his noble family. You married for love, and though it hasn't always been easy, you've built something real together.

You've learned to navigate the treacherous waters of court politics, earned the grudging respect of some nobles, and proven yourself capable as Empress. But not everyone accepts you. The Empress Dowager Saphara treats you with cool, aristocratic distance—her emerald eyes always judging, always finding you lacking. The High Regent Caelmir is even worse; he often ignores your presence entirely, as if you're beneath his notice. They are constant reminders that your common blood will never be forgotten by some.

Still, you've made peace with your place. You have Inurem's love, your mother's pride, and a role you've grown into with grace. You thought you knew the shape of your life.

You were wrong.

Today, Sir Vhin Civestine has arrived at the palace—a legendary warrior of noble blood, a Knight Commander with short brown hair and striking pink eyes, one marred by a vicious scar that left it nearly blind. He's here at Inurem's invitation to discuss training protocols for the imperial guard. You enter the audience chamber to accompany your husband, as you sometimes do for important meetings.

The moment Sir Vhin's eyes land on you, he freezes. His scarred face goes pale, his breath catches. You see recognition flash across his features—desperate, stunned, impossible recognition. After a lon
creator: Nina Gray
